spec_match.rationale: Shows the replacement position-area property and the legacy inset-area spelling against equivalent anchor layouts, with current Chrome 148 reporting inset-area as removed/not parsed.

interactive.rationale: Six direction buttons update both panels, code snippets, computed-property readouts, and anchor offsets after each click.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page copy now promises a direct old/new spelling comparison and the demo renders that comparison inline.

edge_cases.rationale: The legacy panel handles both possible migration-window outcomes: an accepted alias with matching computed output, or the current removed/not-parsed state with no layout effect.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables are used throughout, local button colors set foreground and background, and readouts wrap long property values without horizontal overflow at narrow width.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, the page only drove position-area and did not compare the legacy spelling. After the fix, http://localhost:3000/v131/deprecation-of-css-anchor-positioning-property-inset-area/inset-area-removal/?verify=after-rename-comparison shows side-by-side inset-area and position-area panels, an inline migration/deprecation signal, computed readouts, and anchor offsets. Clicking bottom left and right updated the replacement panel while the legacy panel correctly showed inset-area as not parsed in this browser. The feature conformance route at /v131/deprecation-of-css-anchor-positioning-property-inset-area/conformance/?verify=after-inset-area-removal-comparison reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL with clean console and network.
